title: 10 best herbal teas for body odor

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause body odor, reducing the intensity of unpleasant smells.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin and sweat glands, leading to a decrease in body odor.

Antiperspirant properties: Lemon balm's natural antiperspirant compounds, such as citral, can help to reduce sweating by constricting pores and inhibiting the production of sweat, further reducing body odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, teas helps with body odor because of its potent antibacterial and antifungal properties. The high levels of citral and geranial in Melissa officinalis inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing sweat and body odor. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties soothe the skin, preventing irritation and infections that can contribute to unpleasant body odor. By balancing skin pH and reducing bacterial growth, Melissa officinalis teas provide a natural solution to body odor.
remedy_constituents: Citral: A naturally occurring terpene, citral helps with body odor by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that contribute to sweat's unpleasant smell.
Geraniol: A monoterpenoid compound, geraniol has antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the population of odor-causing bacteria on the skin.
Rosmarinicine: A phenolic compound, rosmarinicine acts as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent, helping to neutralize and eliminate odor-causing substances produced by bacteria in sweat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their calming and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: They are used to make teas due to their sweet, lemony flavor and potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: They are used to make teas due to their ability to be a source of the herb's bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure freshness.
Dry the harvested melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator for 1-2 hours.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water to prepare the tea.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea and serve it hot or chilled within 30 minutes to 1 hour of preparation.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antibacterial Properties: Tea tree oil has potent antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria that cause body odor, such as Staphylococcus aureus and Brevibacterium linens.

Antifungal Properties: The antifungal properties of tea tree oil can help control the growth of fungi like Malassezia furfur, which contributes to body odor by breaking down sweat and releasing foul-smelling compounds.

Deodorizing and Antiperspirant Effects: The essential oils present in tea tree teas have natural deodorizing and antiperspirant properties that help reduce sweat production and eliminate body odor-causing bacteria and fungi.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, teas helps with body odor because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in tea tree oil, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to body odor. By reducing the bacterial load on the skin, tea tree tea can help to prevent the breakdown of sweat into compounds that give rise to unpleasant body odor. This natural remedy can be a useful addition to a personal hygiene routine.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin.
Limonene: This terpene has natural deodorizing and antiseptic properties, which can help to eliminate or reduce unpleasant body odors.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth that can cause body odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to combat body odor-causing bacteria.
Stems: Contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing sweat and body odor.
Barks: Have astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce sweat and prevent the growth of odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 melaleuca alternifolia branches with plenty of leaves for a strong tea flavor.
Dry the fresh branches in a warm shaded area for 1-2 weeks to remove moisture.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving in a separate cup.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild geranium tea's antimicrobial compounds help to eliminate bacteria that contribute to body odor, reducing sweat and its accompanying unpleasant smell.
Astringent properties: The astringent nature of wild geranium helps to constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of moisture on the skin that can lead to bacterial growth and bad odors.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild geranium tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with excessive sweating and body odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium maculatum, also known as wild geranium, teas helps with body odor because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as geraniol and linalool, help to balance the body's natural pH levels, reducing the growth of bacteria that cause body odor. Additionally, its astringent properties help to tighten pores, preventing sweat from accumulating and contributing to bad body odor. This natural remedy promotes a fresher and cleaner body, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic approach to body odor management.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing the growth of bacteria on the skin that cause body odor.
Iridoids: Iridoids, such as geraniin, have been found to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth that leads to body odor.
Tannins: Tannins in Geranium maculatum have astringent properties, which help to reduce sweat and dry out the skin, making it less prone to body odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Geranium maculatum are commonly used to make teas for body odor due to their astringent and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Geranium maculatum are used in teas for body odor as they contain compounds that help to eliminate bacteria and reduce sweat.
Flowers: The flowers of Geranium maculatum are used in teas for body odor due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers and leaves of the herb at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place or using a dehydrator for 24 hours.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers per 8 oz of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes in a covered teapot to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup for consumption.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Summer savory tea's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate bacteria that cause body odor, reducing the intensity and frequency of sweat-related smells.

Deodorizing properties: The volatile oils present in summer savory tea have natural deodorizing properties that neutralize and absorb moisture, reducing sweat buildup and subsequent bacterial growth that causes body odor.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Summer savory tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by sweating, which can contribute to unpleasant body odors.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Satureja hortensis, also known as summer savory, teas helps with body odor because of its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, including carvacrol and thymol, work to combat the bacteria that cause body odor, such as Bifidobacterium and Staphylococcus. By inhibiting the growth of these bacteria, the tea helps to reduce the production of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) responsible for the pungent smell. This results in fresher breath and reduced body odor.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This phenolic compound exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin, thereby reducing body odor.
Thymol: Similar to carvacrol, thymol has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for body odor, promoting a fresher and cleaner body smell.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpenoid has been found to have antifungal properties, which can help control fungal growth on the skin that may contribute to body odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Satureja hortensis leaves are used to make teas for body odor because they contain oils that have antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce bacteria that cause body odor.

Flowers: Satureja hortensis flowers are used to make teas for body odor because they contain essential oils that have deodorizing and antiseptic properties, helping to eliminate body odor-causing bacteria and reduce sweat.

Buds: Satureja hortensis buds are used to make teas for body odor because they contain aromatic compounds that help to reduce body odor by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause unpleasant smells.
remedy_recipe: Harvest satureja hortensis leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom to ensure optimal flavor and aroma.
Dry the harvested satureja hortensis leaves and flowers in a warm place for 1-2 weeks.
Crush 1 tablespoon of dried satureja hortensis leaves and flowers using a mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Steep 1 teaspoon of crushed satureja hortensis in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it immediately to enjoy its flavor and potential health benefits.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea has antimicrobial properties that help to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can cause body odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the sweat glands, which can contribute to body odor issues.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Tasmanian Blue Gum tea help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin and sweat glands, leading to body odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, teas helps with body odor because of its natural deodorizing properties. The tea's primary active compound, eucalyptol, has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause body odor. Additionally, the tea's astringent and antiseptic properties help to dry out and balance the skin's pH, making it less hospitable to the microorganisms that contribute to body odor. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus tea may help to reduce body odor and promote overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a natural antimicrobial agent, cineole helps reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, thereby minimizing the production of sweat and body odor-causing compounds.
α-Pinene: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and fungi causing body odor, while also reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy skin environment.

Catechin: A type of phenolic compound, catechin has potent antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help eliminate odor-causing bacteria and reduce the production of volatile compounds contributing to body odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are used to make teas for body odor due to their deodorizing properties, which help reduce sweat and kill bacteria.
Barks: Barks of Eucalyptus globulus are used to make teas for body odor due to their antimicrobial properties, which help inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria.
Stems: Stems of Eucalyptus globulus are used to make teas for body odor due to their deodorizing properties, which help reduce sweat and kill bacteria, similar to the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh or 15g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Rinse the leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves in 250ml of boiling water.
Infuse for 5 to 7 minutes or until the desired flavor and aroma are achieved.
Strain the tea and serve immediately while still warm and fragrant.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: English Lavender tea has antimicrobial properties that help to kill bacteria that cause body odor, reducing the spread of malodorous compounds and leaving skin smelling fresh.
Deodorizing properties: The tea's deodorizing properties help to neutralize and eliminate sweat, making it an effective natural remedy for reducing body odor and leaving a pleasant scent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with body odor because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce sweat and prevent the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin. remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce bacteria that cause body odor.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial properties that help to reduce sweat and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause body odor.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the late morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the ground powder in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve immediately.

remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Lemongrass tea's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to combat the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the occurrence of body odor-causing sweat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, teas helps with body odor because of its natural antiseptic and deodorizing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh leaves and stalks in the morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to release their oils and flavor.
Steep the chopped material in 1 quart of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to infuse the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a container to remove the solids.
Store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause body odor, promoting a healthier and more fragrant skin environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties in pot marigold tea can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with body odor.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried calendula officinalis flowers from a reputable herbal supplier.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that cause body odor, such as Staphylococcus epidermidis and Corynebacterium spp.

Rinse the harvested absinthe plant material in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of the absinthe plant material in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the absinthe tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer to remove loose plant material and sediment.
Serve the absinthe tea hot or iced and let it steep for an additional 2-3 minutes if desired.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth immediately after steeping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme teas helps with body odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. Strain the mixture and discard solids, then add honey and lemon to taste according to personal preference.
Drink the warm tea three times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate body odor symptoms naturally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with body odor because of its natural antiperspirant properties. The herb's active compounds, such as isovaleric acid and valerinic acid, work together to reduce sweat production and neutralize bacteria that cause unpleasant odors. Additionally, valerian root has antimicrobial properties that help eliminate existing odor-causing microorganisms on the skin, leaving you with a fresher and more confident feeling. By promoting healthy sweating and eliminating odor-causing bacteria, herbal valerian teas can effectively combat body odor and leave you smelling great all day long.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Valerian tea's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, which can contribute to body odor.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort that can be associated with body odor.

Antifungal and antibacterial compounds: Valerian tea contains antifungal and antibacterial compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and isovaleric acid, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valerenic acid, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat body odor-causing bacteria.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have a higher concentration of essential oils than roots, providing a stronger antimicrobial effect against sweat-related odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and leaves in the morning for maximum potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried valerian root or two teaspoons of fresh valerian root with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea, releasing their antimicrobial properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids and preserve the delicate flavor and aroma.
Drink one cup of the warm tea after meals twice daily for two weeks to help reduce body odor and promote healthy digestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with body odor because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has antibacterial properties. remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Licorice root has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against various bacteria, including those that cause body odor, helping to reduce the growth of these microorganisms and their impact on sweat.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal teas because they contain glycyrrhizin, an active compound that helps to eliminate body odor-causing bacteria and reduce sweat.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes from the licorice plant are used in medicinal teas due to their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause body odor, promoting a healthy and fresh-smelling skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots for the tea preparation.
Rinse 5-7 cups of water over the dried roots in a heat-resistant teapot to combine.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes at 212°f (100°c) to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Allow the tea to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew teas helps with body odor because of its natural antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which effectively combat bacteria and fungi that cause unpleasant body smells. remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse their properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup.
Add one tablespoon of honey and mix well for improved palatability.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation, ideally before bedtime for maximum effectiveness.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to body odor that are treated with herbal teas are those related to sweat and bodily secretions. For instance, tea tree oil is often used to treat excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis), while sage tea can help alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS). Other herbs like peppermint and chamomile may be used to soothe digestive issues such as bloating and gas, which are also related to bodily secretions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for body odor are a natural remedy that helps to reduce sweat and alleviate body odor by balancing the body's pH levels, controlling bacteria growth, and improving overall skin health. The best herbal teas for body odor include Cymbopogon citratus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Cymbopogon nardus, Thymus vulgaris, Artemisia absinthium, and Santalum album. If left untreated, body odor can negatively impact social lives, causing feelings of embarrassment, low self-esteem, and anxiety, ultimately affecting daily routines and relationships.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with body odor is a blend of Peppermint, Sage, and Tea Tree. Peppermint helps to reduce sweat and improve digestion, while Sage aids in balancing body chemistry and eliminating bacteria that cause odor. Tea Tree, with its antimicrobial properties, further kills bacteria that contribute to body odor, leaving you feeling fresh and confident throughout the day. Drinking a combination of these teas may help to naturally alleviate body odor concerns.
